commit.message:
Build WASM natives in docs workflow instead of downloading (#3634)

* Build WASM natives in docs workflow instead of downloading

Replace the docs-download-output step (which downloads ALL platform
NuGet packages from CI feed) with a Docker-based WASM native build
that produces only the two .a files needed for the Gallery Blazor app.

Changes:
- Add Docker WASM build step using build-local.sh (Emscripten 3.1.56,
  variant _wasmeh,st)
- Add submodules: recursive to checkout (needed for native build)
- Remove docs-download-output, version detection, samples-generate,
  and NuGet source steps (no longer needed)
- Publish Gallery directly from source tree instead of generated
  samples-preview copy (project already has ProjectReferences)
- Remove git_branch workflow_dispatch input (was only used for CI
  feed download)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: add execute permission to build-local.sh

The CI runner fails with 'Permission denied' because the script
was committed with mode 100644 instead of 100755.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use 'bash' to invoke build-local.sh instead of relying on +x

Avoids permission issues since Windows may strip the execute bit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Strip platform TFMs when publishing Gallery Blazor

The Blazor WASM project only needs net10.0, but SkiaSharp.csproj
multi-targets with android/ios/etc. Setting PlatformTargetFrameworks
to empty avoids requiring those workloads on the docs runner.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix output dir ownership after Docker WASM build

Docker runs as root, creating output/ owned by root. The runner
user then cannot create gallery-publish/ inside it. Fix by chowning
the output directory back to the runner user after the build.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: SkiaSharp
repository.description: SkiaSharp is a cross-platform 2D graphics API for .NET platforms based on Google's Skia Graphics Library. It provides a comprehensive 2D API that can be used across mobile, server and desktop models to render images.

commit.message:
fix(catala): use 'integer of <decimal>' for floor conversion in P071/P072

'integer_of_decimal' does not exist in Catala 1.1.0 --no-stdlib.
The correct type-cast syntax mirrors 'decimal of <integer>':
  decimal of x   -- converts integer x to decimal
  integer of x   -- converts decimal x to integer (truncates toward zero)

Replaces all 'integer_of_decimal of <var>' with 'integer of <var>'
in both ga_3_1_1 and ga_3_1_2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(catala): declare decimal helper variables as context output in P071

Align with pattern used in ga_3_1_2 (loenindeholdelse): all variables
computed by definition rules within a scope must be 'context output',
not bare 'context' (which is reserved for external inputs).

Changed to 'context output':
  - betalingsevneÅrDecimal
  - råydelseMånedDecimal
  - gulvKvotient
  - gulvKvotientInt (was already output)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(catala): correct integer_of_decimal syntax in P071 afdragsordninger

Catala 1.1.0: integer_of_decimal requires a simple variable atom as argument,
NOT a parenthesised expression. Fix: introduce three intermediate decimal
context variables (betalingsevneÅrDecimal, råydelseMånedDecimal, gulvKvotient)
and apply integer_of_decimal <variable> to each.

Broken pattern (rejected by parser):
  integer_of_decimal (decimal of x / decimal of y)

Correct pattern:
  definition xDecimal equals decimal of x / decimal of y
  definition xInt equals integer_of_decimal xDecimal

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(images): bump magnum image for magnum-cluster-api v0.36.2

Signed-off-by: Mohammed Naser <mnaser@vexxhost.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Sprint 12 QA — Wizard API, confirm(), EntityTable actions, CLI await

Critical bug fixes found during QA review of Sprint 12:

Wizard API mismatch (pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Rewrote all wizard steps from { title, fields } to { label, render(data) }
- Wizard expects render functions returning HTML with data-field attrs
- Added initialData, validate functions, proper select rendering

confirm() signature (pc-lcm, pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Changed confirm('string') to confirm({ title, message, ... })
- confirm() destructures an object param, not a string

EntityTable actions (pc-lcm, pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Actions onClick receives single item from row menu, not array
- Wrapped single items in arrays: onClick: (item) => handler([item])

CLIService (alertCmd):
- Added async/await to state.update call in alert resolve

StateEngine seed data:
- Fixed proj-001 vpcs reference from 'Dev-VPC' to 'vpc-002' (UUID)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Track OpenSSF Scorecards for all repos (#126)

Signed-off-by: Jessica G <jwagantall@linuxfoundation.org>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Separate per-client env config and rename "hosted" mode to "m365" (#8)

* Initial plan

* feat: separate env config per client, rename hosted to m365

- Split AppSettings into CoreSettings (core) + ApiSettings, ConsoleSettings, M365Settings (each package)
- Each client settings class reads from its own env file: .env.api, .env.console, .env.m365
- Created .env.api.example, .env.console.example, .env.m365.example; updated root .env.example to redirect
- Renamed AuthContext.hosted_user_token -> user_token; mode check now uses mode != "local"
- Renamed all hosted references to m365: create_hosted_app -> create_m365_app, _get_hosted_user_token -> _get_m365_user_token, _build_hosted_* -> _build_m365_*
- Changed auth_mode="hosted" to auth_mode="m365" in M365 app; auth_mode="api" in FastAPI app
- Renamed test_hosted_app.py -> test_m365_oauth_runtime.py with updated test names
- Updated README, docs/architecture.md, docs/m365-guide.md, docs/api-guide.md, docs/console-guide.md
- Updated .github/copilot-instructions.md and .github/instructions/ files
- All 107 tests pass, lint clean

commit.message:
Add KQL engine, query telemetry, DML commands, vector search, and Explorer enhancements

New features:
- KQL query engine with operators (where, project, extend, summarize, sort, top, take, distinct, count)
- Query telemetry store and activity tracking interfaces and storage implementations
- DML command service for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations
- Vector embedding policy and vector index models
- KQL query controller and query telemetry controller in Host

Explorer (React):
- Container settings panel
- Import documents dialog
- KQL query editor component
- Query telemetry dashboard

Query engine improvements:
- Additional system functions and scalar subquery support
- Enhanced query explain service

Tests:
- KQL query executor tests
- DML command service, consistency middleware, indexing directive tests
- System function, scalar subquery, trigger execution, UDF query tests
- Vector search, unique key, query telemetry, TTL cleanup tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add support for selecting between SurrealDb, SQLite, and InMemory storage
backends via a side config file (emulator-config.json), CLI --storage
option, or the Explorer settings page.

Configuration:
- Add StorageType enum (SurrealDb, Sqlite, InMemory)
- Add Storage property to EmulatorOptions
- Load emulator-config.json from executable directory at startup
- Add --storage CLI option to the start command
- Priority: appsettings.json < emulator-config.json < CLI args

Storage backends:
- InMemoryDocumentStore: ConcurrentDictionary-based, ephemeral
- SqliteDocumentStore: Microsoft.Data.Sqlite, single-file persistence
- SqliteChangeFeedProvider: persistent change feed in SQLite
- InMemory/SQLite activity and query telemetry stores
- DocumentStoreHelpers: shared logic for partition keys, patches, etc.
- StorageServiceRegistration: DI dispatch based on StorageType

Explorer settings UI:
- Storage Configuration card with backend dropdown and data directory
- GET/PUT /api/emulator/config endpoint for emulator-config.json
- Restart-required messaging after saving storage changes
- Storage type shown in the Configuration info card

commit.message:
#34950 Cleanup nano refetch commands in the background (#42472)

<!-- Add the related story/sub-task/bug number, like Resolves #123, or
remove if NA -->
**Related issue:** Resolves #34950

I changed from the original spec of 100 old commands to 3 due to load
test results. Admittedly my load test meant a very large number of hosts
all checked in and triggered deletion at once but at 100 per host and
per command the load was too high. 3 still results in cleanup over time
and doesn't seem to cause load issues.

# Checklist for submitter

If some of the following don't apply, delete the relevant line.

- [x] Changes file added for user-visible changes in `changes/`,
`orbit/changes/` or `ee/fleetd-chrome/changes`.
See [Changes
files](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/guides/committing-changes.md#changes-files)
for more information.

- [x] Input data is properly validated, `SELECT *` is avoided, SQL
injection is prevented (using placeholders for values in statements), JS
inline code is prevented especially for url redirects, and untrusted
data interpolated into shell scripts/commands is validated against shell
metacharacters.
- [x] If paths of existing endpoints are modified without backwards
compatibility, checked the frontend/CLI for any necessary changes

## Testing

- [x] Added/updated automated tests
- [x] Where appropriate, [automated tests simulate multiple hosts and
test for host
isolation](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/reference/patterns-backend.md#unit-testing)
(updates to one hosts's records do not affect another)

- [x] QA'd all new/changed functionality manually

---------

commit.message:
fix(catala): use Include directives so catala typecheck/interpret take one file

catala typecheck and catala interpret only accept a single FILE argument.
Multi-file compilation is not supported via CLI args.

Fix:

  file so the test file pulls in its source file automatically
- Simplify all typecheck and interpret invocations to pass only the test
  file; the includes handle transitive dependencies

commit.message:
Add fallback tiers for fast model selection

GetFastestModelIdAsync now has three tiers:
1. Lowest billing multiplier (existing behavior)
2. Name-based heuristic (mini/flash/haiku keywords)
3. First available model

Previously, if all models had null Billing, no model was selected
and the session would fall back to the SDK default (which may not
be the fastest). The new fallback chain ensures a model is always
picked explicitly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Lumi
repository.description: A personal agentic desktop assistant powered by GitHub Copilot SDK and Avalonia UI

commit.message:
Speed up suggestion generation with cached session

Instead of creating and disposing a new CopilotSession for every
suggestion request (expensive CLI IPC round-trip each time), reuse
a persistent session. The session is created lazily on first use and
cached for subsequent requests, eliminating the session creation
overhead that made suggestions feel slow.

The cached session is invalidated on reconnection or if a request
fails, so a fresh session is created on next attempt. A semaphore
serializes concurrent suggestion requests to avoid session conflicts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(config): add base path for GitHub Pages deployment (#9)

* Initial plan

* fix(config): add base '/event2spec/' to vite.config.ts for GitHub Pages

Without the base path, all JS/CSS assets were served from /assets/...
instead of /event2spec/assets/..., causing 404 errors and a blank page.

Signed-off-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SebastienDegodez/event2spec/sessions/db87cf21-121a-4f5c-86fb-3688ecc89269

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: audit demo saga — rewrite 3 levels with SV violations

Level 6 (Panteón): was asking about interior lamp → now asks building name visible from street
Level 8 (Casas del Rey): was asking about patio sundial → now asks museum name on facade
Level 12 (Tesoro Final): was asking historical trivia → now asks about visible statue features

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

📝 docs: add ADR-009 message origin tracking for pipeline correlation (#69)

Introduces the `origin` field on Message to enable end-to-end tracing
across multi-agent pipelines. Triggered by the issue-triage workflow
brainstorm where fan-out/fan-in patterns need correlation.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UFzC3k3E4s9sBZwnAza7zd

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: loom
repository.description: A local-first agent runtime. Agents are processes. All state lives in the filesystem. Nothing is hidden.

commit.message:
fix: skip button always clickable + SV retry on level change + accessibility rules

- Fix: StreetViewExplorer now retries full init when panoRef is null (previous level failed)
- Fix: Skip button z-index 9999 + pointer-events:auto to stay above overlays
- Rule: Permanent Street View accessibility rules in AI prompt, docs, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md
- AI prompt now has REGLA DE ORO with explicit PROHIBIDO/PERMITIDO lists

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: always-visible skip button + tighter SV radius to reject sea/aerial panoramas

- Skip button always visible (subtle when SV works, prominent when no coverage)
- Reduced SV search radius from 500m to 300m to avoid distant sea panoramas
- Added pano location validation before accepting a panorama
- Handles AI-generated sagas with bad coordinates gracefully

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(bitwarden-android): align sdk-internal and drop drift shims

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: relax saga RLS to allow playing non-active-status sagas

- Migration 005: allow viewing sagas with is_active=true (not just status='active')
- Better error messages in play API route
- Parse API error body in play page for clearer user feedback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

app: add ytdlnis source build package

Pin ytdlnis to latest upstream commit and add Gradle deps lockfile.

repository.name: repo
repository.description: build script for android operating system and android apps.

commit.message:
fix: handle no Street View coverage with skip + satellite fallback

- Increase SV search radius from 200m to 500m
- Show satellite map fallback when no SV coverage
- Add 'Saltar misión' skip button on play page
- Reset no-coverage state on level change
- Improve Gemini prompt to prefer main streets and landmarks
- Add onNoCoverage callback to StreetViewExplorer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add level system, combo scoring, REPAIR_KIT, high score, block-hit feedback

- Level system: every 3 demand deliveries advance the level; fuel drain
  increases per level; hazard/block spawn weights scale up each level;
  LVL label shown in HUD.
- Combo scoring: consecutive resource pickups earn +4 pts per combo tier
  (capped at x5); hazard/block hits reset combo; combo label shown in HUD.
- REPAIR_KIT powerup (cyan cross item): restores 1 durability, weight 6;
  added to ItemType enum, spawner weights, and legend.
- High score: persisted to user://save.cfg via ConfigFile; shown on game
  over screen.
- Block-hit feedback: truck flashes red + shake tween on block hit; red
  screen-flash overlay in HUD; durability bar pulses red.
- Fuel warning: pulsing red animation on fuel bar when below 25%.
- Delivery banner: centred on-screen banner for deliveries and level-ups.
- Fuel drops increased from 5% to ~12% spawn weight.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix TUI runtime flow and setup dependency handling

- improve Rust backend startup path resolution and error surfacing
- wire quit_requested and preserve quiz-local Q behavior
- integrate shared UI helpers across key screens
- remove unused backend protocol wrappers
- avoid setuptools upgrade churn that conflicts with torch
- install only missing/incompatible Python requirements
- auto-detect Rust binary path for symlink creation
- update README hotkey/setup notes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix invalid Array * int operator in item_base.gd

Replace [col.lightened(0.4)] * 4 with explicit PackedColorArray
to fix 'invalid operands Array and init for * operator' error.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix FFmpeg lavfi syntax for background generation

- Remove filter_complex labels when using -f lavfi
- Use direct filter chain without [base] and [out] labels
- Fixes 'Output with label out does not exist' error
